vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but shows significant degradation. P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) is present. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are in the black pot, but show severe decline.

plant_audit.P1: String of Nickels: Stable, minimal growth, leaf color remains consistent.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Mexican Mint/Silver Guest: Critical failure. The central green leaf observed in early images has undergone complete necrosis and abscission.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos: The primary leaf remains, but the surrounding soil shows signs of desiccation. The rabbit anchor is stable.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dry, granular, with visible perlite/debris. No signs of fungal bloom.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Accumulation of organic debris (dried leaf fragments) noted near the base of the pots.

temporal_deltas.Earliest_to_T-4: Minor leaf wilting observed on the P2/P4 specimen.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-2: Progressive chlorosis and postural collapse of the P2/P4 leaf; it transitioned from upright to horizontal.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_Current: Total loss of the P2/P4 leaf structure. The pot now contains only soil and debris.

anomalies: Presence of white, shell-like debris in the P2/P4 pot, likely remnants of a failed propagation or organic matter breakdown.
Complete disappearance of the primary leaf in the P2/P4 pot between T-2 and T-1.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of decline. While the String of Nickels (P1) and the Pothos (P3) maintain a baseline level of survival, the P2/P4 container has suffered a total systemic loss of its primary specimen. The progression from a healthy, upright leaf to total disappearance suggests either rapid necrosis or physical removal of the dead tissue. The environment is currently too dry to support active growth.
